skill.name: csctf
skill.description: Convert shared ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude conversations into clean Markdown and static HTML transcripts with preserved code blocks and stable filenames.
skill.summary: Export shared AI conversations to Markdown and static HTML transcripts.
skill.author: Dicklesworthstone

security_audit.summary: Static findings are mixed: many Ruby backtick and weak-crypto alerts are Markdown false positives, not executable code. However, the skill documents copying Chrome session cookies, launching Chrome with remote debugging, publishing through GitHub tooling, and installing via curl piped to bash. These behaviors are legitimate for the stated CLI but create high risk for credential exposure and supply-chain compromise.

high_findings.title: Browser Session Cookie Handling
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents copying Chrome session cookies to a temporary profile and using Chrome DevTools Protocol for Claude extraction. This is sensitive credential material, and compromise of the temporary profile or debugging session could expose authenticated browser access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented Claude flow explicitly copies Chrome session cookies and launches Chrome with remote debugging. The stated purpose is legitimate, but the credential exposure risk is direct.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The installation instructions run a remote install script through bash. This pattern gives the fetched script immediate execution capability and depends on transport, repository, and script integrity at install time.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pipe-to-shell pattern appears verbatim in the quick start and installation sections. It is documentation, not bundled executable code, but users are instructed to run it.

medium_findings.title: Automated GitHub Publishing Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents creating or cloning a GitHub repository, regenerating files, committing, and pushing through the GitHub CLI. This can modify remote repositories and publish transcripts, so users must verify content and destination settings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented publish flow clearly performs network and repository writes. It is opt-in and confirmed by prompts, which keeps it below high severity.

medium_findings.title: Stealth Browser Automation
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The documented scraper uses headless Chromium with stealth configuration, spoofed browser properties, and Cloudflare challenge handling. This can be legitimate for exporting user-accessible shares, but it increases abuse and terms-of-service risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly names stealth configuration and challenge handling. The scope is limited to public shares and the user browser session, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The large set of Ruby shell-backtick detections is caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, tables, and examples. No Ruby source file or executable backtick expression exists in the scanned skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanned file is Markdown documentation, and the matched backticks are formatting delimiters or shell examples. They are not Ruby code execution constructs.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal And Weak Crypto Alerts Lack Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The path traversal alerts correspond to abbreviated installer examples and paths such as install script placeholders. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not identify an implemented cryptographic operation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is documentation text and command examples, not implementation code. I found no concrete file traversal routine or cryptographic API usage in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Pipe To bash Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation recommends fetching an installer from GitHub and piping it directly to bash, which is a supply-chain execution risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pattern appears in both quick start and installation sections. It is risky even though it is user-invoked documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Chrome Cookie Copy And Debugging Session
dangerous_patterns.description: The Claude workflow copies Chrome cookies to a temporary profile and launches Chrome with remote debugging enabled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is described directly in the skill text. It touches authenticated browser state and therefore has a clear security impact.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Publishing To Remote Repository
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub Pages flow uses authenticated GitHub CLI operations to create, modify, commit, and push exported transcript files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The publish flow is explicit and requires GitHub CLI authentication. It is opt-in, but it can expose transcript content publicly.

content.user_title: Convert Shared AI Chats to Markdown and HTML
content.value_statement: Shared AI conversations are difficult to archive without losing formatting. This skill documents a CLI that exports public chat links into Markdown and HTML.
actual_capabilities: Converts public ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude share links into Markdown transcripts.
Generates static HTML versions with inline styling and no emitted JavaScript.
Preserves fenced code blocks and language hints when extracting conversations.
Creates deterministic filenames with collision handling and Windows reserved-name protection.
Supports optional GitHub Pages publishing through the GitHub CLI.
Documents Claude-specific extraction through a logged-in Chrome session.
limitations: Requires public share links, except Claude exports that use a local Chrome login.
Provider layout changes can break selectors until the CLI is updated.
First use may download a Playwright Chromium browser bundle.
Claude support requires Chrome installed and logged into claude.ai.

use_cases.title: Archive research conversations
use_cases.description: Save public AI research chats as readable Markdown and HTML files for later review.

use_cases.title: Publish selected support transcripts
use_cases.description: Create static transcript pages that can be hosted on GitHub Pages after review.

use_cases.title: Preserve coding examples
use_cases.description: Export conversations that contain code while keeping fenced blocks and language labels.

prompt_templates.title: Export one public chat
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion of a single shared conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use csctf to convert this public share link into Markdown and HTML. Save the files with the default deterministic filename.

prompt_templates.title: Create Markdown only
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a lightweight archive for notes or search.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this shared AI conversation to Markdown only. Keep the output quiet and place it in my archive folder.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a static transcript page
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate an HTML version for internal documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Export this shared conversation as HTML only. Use a clear filename and confirm that the output contains no emitted JavaScript.

prompt_templates.title: Publish reviewed transcripts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with GitHub Pages publishing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this approved public share link and publish it to the configured GitHub Pages repository. Show the destination before pushing.

output_examples.input: A public ChatGPT share link containing Python examples.
output_examples.output: A Markdown transcript and matching HTML page with preserved Python code blocks and a stable filename.

output_examples.input: A Gemini share link for a long research discussion.
output_examples.output: A clean transcript with user and assistant sections, normalized spacing, source metadata, and printable HTML.

output_examples.input: An approved transcript that should be published.
output_examples.output: A regenerated GitHub Pages index, committed transcript files, and a viewer URL after confirmation.

best_practices: Review transcript content before publishing it to a public GitHub Pages site.
Prefer pinned versions and checksum verification instead of direct pipe-to-shell installation.
Use a separate browser profile for Claude exports when handling sensitive conversations.
anti_patterns: Do not export private or confidential conversations to public repositories.
Do not run the remote installer without reviewing the script source and integrity.
Do not keep temporary browser profiles or copied session data longer than needed.

faq.question: What providers does this skill describe?
faq.answer: It describes exports for ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude shared conversations.

faq.question: Does it work with private share links?
faq.answer: No. The documented flow expects public share links, except Claude exports that use your local Chrome session.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The documented Claude flow handles browser cookies, and the install instructions include remote scripts executed by bash.

faq.question: Does the generated HTML require JavaScript?
faq.answer: The documentation states that generated HTML is static and emits no JavaScript.

faq.question: Can it publish exports online?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents an opt-in GitHub Pages workflow using the authenticated GitHub CLI.

faq.question: What should users check before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should verify the transcript content, repository destination, branch, directory, and public visibility.
